summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened his Christmas Eve message by comparing the childhood game of hide-and-seek to the spiritual condition of many adults. He observed that while children hide hoping to be found, adults often hide from God, hoping to stay concealed in the darkness of sin, shame, pride, addiction, and other strongholds. This hiding leads to isolation and a loss of identity. The core message, titled 'Come Out Wherever You Are,' is that Christmas represents God stepping into human darkness not to condemn but to rescue. Pastor Travis presented three hard truths: first, hiding from God does not bring freedom but imprisonment; second, it is possible to live in spiritual darkness while believing you are in the light, deceived by desires that Satan baits like a fisherman; and third, the light of Christ comes not to expose and shame but to save and heal. He emphasized that God is a seeking Father who calls out, 'Where are you?' not for information but for transformation, inviting us to step out of the shadows and into His wonderful light. The sermon concluded with a powerful call to salvation and a reminder that darkness does not get the final word.
mainPoints: Hiding from God does not make you free; it imprisons you and costs you connection.
You can live in spiritual darkness and think you are living in the light, deceived by Satan who masquerades as an angel of light.
The light of Christ comes not to expose and condemn you, but to save and heal you, calling you out of darkness into relationship.

versesMentioned.text: I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.

This is the verdict: Light has come into the world, but people loved darkness instead of light because their deeds were evil. Everyone who does evil hates the light, and will not come into the light for fear that their deeds will be exposed.
But the Lord God called to the man, 'Where are you?'
He answered, 'I heard you in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; so I hid.'
And no wonder, for Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light.
but each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed.
This is how God showed his love among us: He sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.
The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
But you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God’s special possession, that you may declare the praises of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.

If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved. For it is with your heart that you believe and are justified, and it is with your mouth that you profess your faith and are saved.

Monday.scripture: John 8:12 (NIV) - 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.'
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you feel you are 'hiding' or walking in spiritual darkness. Simply acknowledge it to God.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being the light of the world. Help me to be honest with you today about where I am hiding. Give me the courage to turn toward your light. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: Genesis 3:9 (NIV) - 'But the Lord God called to the man, "Where are you?"'
Tuesday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet, listening prayer. Ask God, 'Where am I?' and be open to His gentle conviction.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, you are a seeking God. I hear you calling 'Where are you?' Help me to answer you honestly and to step out of the shadows of my own making. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 11:14 (NIV) - 'And no wonder, for Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light.'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on a desire or habit that feels 'good' or 'normal' but may be pulling you from God. Ask the Holy Spirit for clarity.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me discernment. Help me to see any bait in my life that looks like light but leads to darkness. I choose to follow the true light, Jesus. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: James 1:14 (NIV) - '...each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed.'
Thursday.action: Write down one personal desire that often leads you into temptation. Commit to bringing this desire into God's light through prayer whenever it arises.
Thursday.prayer: God, my desires can lead me astray. I bring my desire for [name it] before you today. Help me to find my ultimate satisfaction in you, not in the enemy's bait. Amen.

Friday.scripture: 1 Peter 2:9 (NIV) - '...him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.'
Friday.action: Declare out loud: 'I am called out of darkness into God's wonderful light.' Let this truth shape your choices and conversations today.
Friday.prayer: Thank you, God, for calling me out. I am yours—chosen and loved. Help me to live today in the freedom and identity of being a child of your light. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: 1 John 4:9 (NIV) - 'This is how God showed his love among us: He sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.'
Saturday.action: Perform one act of kindness for someone, motivated purely by God's love for you, not by fear or obligation.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, you came not to condemn but to save. Your love casts out my fear. Let your love flow through me today as I step out of hiding and live fully for you. Amen.
